How Transformers Become ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Other AI Assistants
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Thumbnail by Gemini :)Key Concept
A Transformer is the architecture.
An LLM (Large Language Model) is a Transformer that has been trained on massive amounts of data and contains billions of learned parameters.
In simple terms:
Transformer = Engine
LLM = Complete Car
From Transformer to LLM
Earlier, we learned that the Transformer is the breakthrough architecture behind modern AI.
But a Transformer by itself doesn’t know anything.
It’s like a newborn brain.
To become useful, it must learn from enormous amounts of information.
Imagine giving a student access to:

• Millions of books

• Research papers

• Websites

• Programming tutorials

• Documentation

• Encyclopedias

• Public conversations
Then asking them to study continuously for years.
That is essentially what happens when building an LLM.
The model learns patterns from massive amounts of text.
After training, it becomes capable of:

• Answering questions

• Writing articles

• Generating code

• Summarizing documents

• Translating languages

• Brainstorming ideas
This trained Transformer is what we call a Large Language Model.
Why Is It Called a “Language Model”?
Let’s break the term into pieces.
Language
The model works with human language.
Examples:

• English

• Tamil

• Hindi

• French

• Programming languages

• Mathematical notation
Model
A model is simply a learned representation of patterns.
Think of a weather forecast.
It doesn’t know the future.
It predicts likely outcomes based on patterns.
Similarly, an LLM predicts likely words based on patterns learned during training.
The Core Job of an LLM
Surprisingly, an LLM performs a very simple task.
Predict the next token.
That’s it.
Everything else emerges from this capability.
Let’s see an example.
Input: “The capital of France is”
The model predicts: “Paris”
Then it predicts the next token.
Then the next… Then the next.
Over and over again.
The capital of France is Paris.What looks like intelligence is actually billions of highly sophisticated next-token predictions happening extremely quickly.

What Is a Token?
Before we continue, we need to understand one of the most important AI concepts.
Tokens Are Not Words
Many beginners assume AI reads words.
It doesn’t.
AI reads tokens.
A token is a small piece of text.
For example: “ Hello ”
might be one token.
But:
Artificial Intelligencemight become:
Artificial
IntelligenceTwo separate tokens.
Sometimes even a single word is split into multiple tokens.
For example:
unbelievablecould become:
un
believ
abledepending on the tokenizer.
Why Use Tokens?
Tokens help models process language more efficiently.
Think of LEGO bricks.
Instead of storing millions of complete words, the model stores smaller reusable pieces.
These pieces can be combined to form new words and sentences.
What Are Parameters?
Another word you’ll hear constantly is:
Parameters
A parameter is a learned value inside the model.
Think of parameters as tiny pieces of knowledge.
One parameter might learn:

• Grammar patterns
Another might learn:

• Relationships between words
Another might learn:

• Coding syntax
Another might learn:

• Mathematical structures

Analogy: Brain Connections
Imagine your brain contains billions of connections between neurons.
Those connections store experience and learning.
Parameters play a similar role in an AI model.
The more parameters a model has:

• The more patterns it can potentially learn

• The more complex relationships it can represent
Examples:
This is where the word Large in LLM comes from.
How Training Works
Training is the process of teaching the model.
Let’s simplify what happens.
Step 1
Show text to the model.
The sky is blue.Step 2
Hide the next token.
The sky is ___Step 3
Ask the model to guess.
green?Wrong.
Step 4
Adjust internal parameters.
Step 5
Repeat trillions of times.
Over time, the model becomes increasingly accurate.
This process can take:

• Weeks

• Months

• Thousands of GPUs

• Millions of dollars
Training modern frontier models is one of the most computationally intensive tasks in technology.
What Is a Context Window?
Imagine talking to someone with very short memory.
You say:
My name is Astra.Five minutes later:
What's my name?They respond:
I don't know.Not very helpful.

LLMs have something called a Context Window.
A context window is the amount of information the model can “see” during a conversation.
Think of it as the model’s temporary workspace.
Previous Messages
+
Current Question
=
Context WindowThe larger the context window:

• More conversation history

• Larger documents

• Longer code files

• Better continuity
can be handled at once.
Analogy
Imagine reading a book through a window.
A small window shows one paragraph.
A larger window shows multiple pages.
The larger the window, the more context you can understand.

What Is Fine Tuning?
Training teaches general knowledge.
Fine tuning teaches specialization.
Think of a medical student.
First:

• Learn biology

• Learn chemistry

• Learn anatomy
General education.
Then:

• Become a cardiologist

• Become a neurologist

• Become a surgeon
Specialized education.
Fine tuning works similarly.
A general LLM can be further trained for:

• Medicine

• Law

• Finance

• Customer support

• Programming
This creates a model specialized for specific tasks.

What Is Inference?
Training is learning.
Inference is using what was learned.
When you ask ChatGPT:
Explain recursion simply.The model is no longer learning.
It is generating an answer.
That generation process is called Inference.
Simple Workflow
User Prompt
↓
Tokenization
↓
Transformer Processing
↓
Predict Next Token
↓
Generate Response
↓
Display OutputEvery ChatGPT conversation follows this workflow.
What Is Temperature?
Temperature controls creativity.
Think of it as a randomness dial.
Low Temperature
More predictable, More focused, More deterministic…
Example:
Question:
What is 2 + 2?Answer:
4High Temperature
More creative, More diverse, More surprising…
Example:
Prompt: Write a fantasy story opening.
The model may generate many different possibilities.

Analogy
Imagine asking five chefs to cook the same dish.
Low temperature:
Everyone follows the recipe exactly.
High temperature:
Each chef adds their own creative twist.
That is essentially what temperature controls.
How ChatGPT Actually Generates Responses
Let’s finally connect everything together.
When you type:
Explain machine learning simply.ChatGPT performs something like this:
Step 1
Convert your prompt into tokens.
Explain
machine
learning
simply↓
Step 2
Pass tokens into the Transformer.
↓
Step 3
Use Self Attention to understand relationships.
↓
Step 4
Predict the next token.
Machine↓
Step 5
Predict another token.
learning↓
Step 6
Repeat hundreds of times.
↓
Step 7
Build a complete response.
All of this happens in seconds.
What feels like a conversation is actually an incredibly sophisticated sequence of next-token predictions powered by a Transformer architecture with billions of parameters.
The Big Picture
At this point, we can finally connect the pieces.
Transformer
↓
Training
↓
Billions of Parameters
↓
Large Language Model
↓
ChatGPT / Gemini / ClaudeThe Transformer provides the architecture.
Training provides knowledge.
Parameters store learned patterns.
The result is an LLM capable of generating human-like language.
But there is still an important problem.
Even the most powerful LLMs can:

• Hallucinate facts

• Miss recent information

• Forget company documents

• Lack access to private knowledge
This limitation led to one of the most important innovations in modern AI systems:
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
Before we get there, we’ll first explore a growing trend in the AI industry:
Small Language Models (SLMs) and why many companies are moving toward smaller, faster, and cheaper AI models.
